---
title: Manifest reference
description: Component metadata lives in the act:component custom section, merged at build time from language manifests and an optional act.toml patch.
---

import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

`act-build pack` writes the `act:component` WASM custom section (CBOR-encoded). Its contents are **merged** from several sources:

1. Language manifest — `Cargo.toml` / `pyproject.toml` / `package.json`
2. SDK-emitted fields — produced by `#[act_component]` / `@component` at build time
3. `act.toml` — sidecar patch, applied last

Later sources override earlier ones (RFC 7396 merge-patch).

## Basic fields

Keys live under `[std]`. Name, version, and description come from the language manifest by default.

<Tabs syncKey="lang">
<TabItem label="Rust">
```toml
# Cargo.toml
[package]
name = "component-sqlite"
version = "0.2.3"
description = "SQLite database"

[package.metadata.act]
# optional overrides/additions
```
</TabItem>
<TabItem label="Python">
```toml
# pyproject.toml
[project]
name = "python-eval"
version = "0.1.1"
description = "Python interpreter"

[tool.act.std]
# optional overrides/additions
```
</TabItem>
<TabItem label="act.toml">
```toml
[std]
name = "sqlite"               # last-wins override of the Cargo / pyproject name
version = "0.2.3"
description = "SQLite database"
```
</TabItem>
</Tabs>

## Capability declarations

A component declares the WASI capabilities it needs. The host uses this as a **ceiling** on what the user's policy can grant: an undeclared capability, or a declaration with an empty `allow` list, is a hard deny regardless of `--fs-allow` or `--http-allow`.

### Filesystem

```toml
[std.capabilities."wasi:filesystem"]
description = "Stores the database file."

[[std.capabilities."wasi:filesystem".allow]]
path = "**"        # glob; "**" means any path
mode = "rw"        # "ro" or "rw"
```

Multiple `[[...allow]]` entries are OR'd. The user's runtime policy is intersected with this ceiling.

### HTTP

```toml
[std.capabilities."wasi:http"]
description = "Fetches OpenAPI specs from public catalogs."

[[std.capabilities."wasi:http".allow]]
host = "petstore3.swagger.io"   # "*" = any host; "*.example.com" = suffix
scheme = "https"                # optional — any scheme if absent
methods = ["GET"]               # optional — any method if absent
ports = [443]                   # optional — standard port for the scheme
```

## Agent skill embedding

If a `skill/` directory exists at the package root, `act-build pack` tars it and embeds it as the `act:skill` custom section. Extract with `act skill <component.wasm> -o out/`. See [Agent Skills](/docs/build/skills/).

## Validation

```bash
act-build validate path/to/component.wasm
```

Checks that declared capabilities parse, that the CBOR section is readable, and that the file is a valid component.

## Merge order cheatsheet

```
┌─────────────────────────┐
│  Cargo / pyproject / package  │   defaults from the language manifest
└─────────────────────────┘
           ▼
┌─────────────────────────┐
│  SDK-emitted fields     │   macros / decorators contribute at compile time
└─────────────────────────┘
           ▼
┌─────────────────────────┐
│  act.toml (sidecar)     │   last wins — use this for per-deploy overrides
└─────────────────────────┘
           ▼
     act:component (CBOR)
```
